## [03:04 - 06:06]

It has nothing whatsoever to do with where you are today. And it actually has very little to do other than being strategic to what you do first. So first thing we have to do is say wherever you are at, clean the slate. Brand new canvas. We're not going to deal with that. So you are somewhere in your life. You have to decide, do I want my entrepreneur activities, my self-employed business ownership, product or service activities to be part-time or full-time? And the reason you have to ask that is you have to decide what strategy will work best for you where you are at right now. For example, if you don't feel comfortable giving up whatever you do by day, whether you work at Starbucks or whether you're a manager in a major corporation. If you're not happy but your life depends on your paycheck right now, you would not be very wise to abandon your job and just throw caution to the wind as we say here and just start a business without really thinking it through. You would be wisely advised to start a part-time business that had a very high success probability, but it didn't make a lot of money and was able to utilize whatever your greatest skill sets are, whether it's your ability on a technology or a process or your motivation or your ability to communicate and compel people to get control of products or services. But you have a lot of options. So for many people, not all, and you'll see this in your template, the first choice is do I do my first entrepreneurial activity part-time?
